content.problemStatement:
A Binary string SSS containing only 000s and 111s is cool if it contains exactly MMM different non-empty subsequences.

Now your task is to find the number of inversions in NNN-th lexicographically maximum cool binary string.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first and only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line :



If there are at least NNN cool binary strings, the number of inversions in the NNN-th lexicographically largest binary string.
−1-1−1 if there are less than NNN cool binary strings

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
All the good strings with exactly 333 distinct subsequences are listed below (in lexicographically descending order) :

111\text{111}111
10\text{10}10
01\text{01}01
000\text{000}000

Test Case 1 : N=3N = 3N=3 and so the required string is 010101. This has 000 inversions.
Test Case 2 : N=2N = 2N=2 and so the required string is 101010. This has 111 inversion.
Test Case 3 : There are lesser than 191919 strings with number of distinct subsequences exactly 101010. Hence, the answer is −1-1−1.
